title: Ensure that Activity Log Alert exists for Create Policy  Assignment

input_row.title: Ensure that Activity Log Alert exists for Create Policy  Assignment
input_row.description: Create an activity log alert for the Create Policy Assignment event.
input_row.rationale: Monitoring for create policy assignment events gives insight into changes done in "Azure policy - assignments" and can reduce the time it takes to detect unsolicited changes.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Navigate to the Monitor blade. 2. Click on Alerts. 3. In the Alerts window, click on Alert rules. 4. Ensure an alert rule exists where the Condition column contains Operation name=Microsoft.Authorization/policyAssignments/write. 5. Click on the Alert Name associated with the previous step. 6. Ensure the Condition panel displays the text Whenever the Activity Log has an event with Category='Administrative', Operation name='Create policy assignment' and does not filter on Level, Status or Caller. 7. Ensure the Actions panel displays an Action group is assigned to notify the appropriate personnel in your organization. Audit from Azure CLI az monitor activity-log alert list --subscription --query "[].{Name:name,Enabled:enabled,Condition:condition.allOf,Actions:actions}" Look for Microsoft.Authorization/policyAssignments/write in the output. If it's missing, generate a finding. Audit from PowerShell Get-AzActivityLogAlert -SubscriptionId |where-object {$_.ConditionAllOf.Equal -match "Microsoft.Authorization/policyAssignments/write"}|select-object Location,Name,Enabled,ResourceGroupName,ConditionAllOf If the output is empty, an alert rule for Create Policy Assignments is not configured. Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: c5447c04-a4d7-4ba8-a263-c9ee321a6858 - Name: 'An activity log alert should exist for specific Policy operations'
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Navigate to the Monitor blade. 2. Select Alerts. 3. Select Create. 4. Select Alert rule. 5. Choose a subscription. 6. Select Apply. 7. Select the Condition tab. 8. Click See all signals. 9. Select Create policy assignment (Policy assignment). 10. Click Apply. 11. Select the Actions tab. 12. Click Select action groups to select an existing action group, or Create action group to create a new action group. 13. Follow the prompts to choose or create an action group. 14. Select the Details tab. 15. Select a Resource group, provide an Alert rule name and an optional Alert rule description. 16. Click Review + create. 17. Click Create. Remediate from Azure CLI az monitor activity-log alert create --resource-group "" --condition category=Administrative and operationName=Microsoft.Authorization/policyAssignments/write> --scope "/subscriptions/" --name "" -- subscription --action-group Remediate from PowerShell Create the conditions object. $conditions = @() $conditions += New-AzActivityLogAlertAlertRuleAnyOfOrLeafConditionObject - Equal Administrative -Field category $conditions += New-AzActivityLogAlertAlertRuleAnyOfOrLeafConditionObject - Equal Microsoft.Authorization/policyAssignments/write -Field operationName Get the Action Group information and store it in a variable, then create a new Action object. $actionGroup = Get-AzActionGroup -ResourceGroupName - Name $actionObject = New-AzActivityLogAlertActionGroupObject -Id $actionGroup.Id Create the Scope variable. $scope = "/subscriptions/" Create the Activity Log Alert Rule for Microsoft.Authorization/policyAssignments/write New-AzActivityLogAlert -Name "" -ResourceGroupName "" -Condition $conditions -Scope $scope -Location global -Action $actionObject -Subscription -Enabled $true
input_row.default_value: By default, no monitoring alerts are created.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_cloudwatch_alert_create_policy_assignment

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
1. **AWS API Calls/Boto3 Methods:**
   - Use `boto3.client('cloudwatch')` to interact with AWS CloudWatch.
   - Check for existing CloudWatch Alarms using `cloudwatch.describe_alarms()` to see if there's an alarm corresponding to policy assignment events.
   
2. **AWS CLI Commands:**
   - Use the CLI command: `aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query "Alarms[?contains(AlarmDescription, 'Create policy assignment')]"`
   
3. **Specific Resources to Enumerate:**
   - The script will enumerate CloudWatch Alarms across all available regions and accounts to identify any alert that matches the condition for policy assignments.

4. **Exact Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
   - Validate the presence of an Alarm with:
     - **Alarm Description** matching 'Create policy assignment' 
     - An appropriate action that defines notification channels for alerting.
   
5. **Pass/Fail Criteria with Expected Values:**
   - **Pass:** At least one CloudWatch Alarm exists with the necessary description and action(s) to notify appropriate personnel about changes in policy assignments.
   - **Fail:** No such Alarm exists, or the Alarm does not trigger actions when policy assignments are created.

The controls outlined follow a clear structure utilized in AWS, simplifying the monitoring of activity logs and ensuring compliance with having alarms for critical events such as policy assignments, thereby increasing the security posture by reducing the detection time for unsolicited changes.
